channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Sunday, October 13 (game #490)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: NYT Strands today — hints, answers and spangram for Sunday, October 13 (game #224)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Peacemaker season 2: HBO Max release date prediction, confirmed cast, possible plot, and more news and rumors
item.description: Here's everything we know about Peacemaker season 2, the DCU Chapter One series that's set to arrive sometime in 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI Scams Targeting Celebrities: A Growing Concern - Exclusive Report
item.description: Celebrities targeted by AI scams, parents warned of kidnapping scheme using voice replicas, US Navy turns to robots and AI for vessel maintenance
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 8 biggest tech news stories from Nintendo's new alarm clock, to Toyota's revolutionary EV charging tech
item.description: ICYMI here's the biggest tech news stories from Nintendo, Toyota, Panasonic, Google Gemini and more for October 12, 2024.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: AI Governance in Government: Ensuring Citizen Safety and Privacy
item.description: Government agencies must adopt best practices to ensure AI-enabled services are safe and reliable for citizens.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Key supplier of this critical semiconductor material is back in operation post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Protect Your Digital Life: Tips for Phone Theft Prevention and Recovery
item.description: Learn how to prevent phone theft, recover stolen devices, and protect your digital life with expert tips and advice.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs
item.description: Michael Berkholtz, a senior manager for technology lifecycle services for GSA, said the agency is analyzing emerging technologies based on four criteria. The post GSA’s emerging tech radar keeping tabs on future needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: How a Cold War plan to stop nuclear proliferation could protect the world from an AI arms race
item.description: As Mark Twain said, “History doesn’t often repeat itself, but it does rhyme.” We are fortunate that it does, because it means we can use lessons from the past at least as a rough guide to the present.
item.dc:creator: Ash Jogalekar, Fast Company

item.title: IBM: 78% of mainframe shops are piloting, operating AI apps
item.description: AI is reinvigorating the mainframe and causing enterprises to rethink their plans for mainframe modernization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Energy leader Petrobras sails toward sustainability with new supply chain solution
item.description: Brazilian oil, natural gas, and energy corporation Petrobras’ longtime mission has been to be the best in its field in terms of value creation with an emphasis on environmental responsibility.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FinOps SaaS: The missing piece of the puzzle
item.description: With more and more businesses moving to the Cloud, FinOps is becoming a vital framework for efficiently controlling Cloud expenses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Say goodbye to dead batteries with this portable wind turbine
item.description: The Shine 2.0 is a 3-in-1 portable wind turbine, charge controller and power bank that fits into a backpack and works day and night, rain or shine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta AI launches in Brazil, U.K., and more markets in a bid to beat ChatGPT
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Wednesday its artificial intelligence chatbot, Meta AI, will be available in 21 new markets, as it looks to compete with OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Julia language adds lower-overhead Memory type
item.description: A new version of the dynamically typed, high-performance Julia language for numerical computing has been released. Julia 1.11 features a new Memory type, a lower-level container that provides an alternative to Array. Downloadable from julialang.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Databricks says with its new Databricks Apps platform, you can build tailored enterprise apps in 5 minutes
item.description: Every enterprise today is looking to get as much as possible out of its data and AI investments. But when building in-house apps, developers can struggle with infrastructure constraints and maintenance, security, data governance, compliance, and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Inflection AI shifts to Intel Gaudi 3, challenging Nvidia’s AI chip lead
item.description: Inflection AI is shifting its latest enterprise platform to Intel’s Gaudi 3 accelerators, stepping away from Nvidia GPUs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: American Water, largest water utility in US, dealing with cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A flying shopping cart could make your future grocery runs that much easier
item.description: A flying shopping cart could revolutionize the way we shop and transport things. The Palletrone is a hovering platform that users push around at chest height.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
